perturbation.original_text: 1.9 "ELEPHANT TALK Customer" shall mean any company or legal entity partnering with ELEPHANT TALK, which wishes to offer mobile services in a MVNO/ESP/SP model using the T-Mobile Network and the by T-Mobile provided Hosting Services under the terms and conditions as mentioned in this Agreement. ELEPHANT TALK Customers might include the mobile end-users of ELEPHANT TALK Customers, subject to the interpretation of the specific provision.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 "ELEPHANT TALK Customer" shall mean any company or legal entity partnering with ELEPHANT TALK, which wishes to offer mobile services in a MVNO/ESP/SP model using the T-Mobile Network and the by T-Mobile provided Hosting Services under the terms and conditions as mentioned in this Agreement. ELEPHANT TALK Customers explicitly exclude the mobile end-users of ELEPHANT TALK Customers, regardless of the interpretation of any other provision.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'ELEPHANT TALK Customer' allows for the inclusion of mobile end-users based on specific provision interpretations. The modified definition explicitly excludes these end-users. This creates an in-text contradiction because certain provisions may assume end-users are included, now creating a conflict with this explicit exclusion, particularly in Section 10 Customer Care Support, where Elephant Talk is responsible for support, potentially including support related to end-users.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 ELEPHANT TALK shall for its own account and at its own cost maintain a customer support organization for ELEPHANT TALK Customers. In addition, T-Mobile shall provide second line customer care support to the back office of ELEPHANT TALK's customer support organization, according to Appendix 1, 2 and 6.
perturbation.justification: The modified text explicitly excludes mobile end-users from the definition of 'ELEPHANT TALK Customer,' while the original definition allowed for their inclusion based on interpretation of specific provisions. Section 10.1 states ELEPHANT TALK must maintain a customer support organization for 'ELEPHANT TALK Customers'. If end-users are explicitly excluded from the definition of 'ELEPHANT TALK Customer' as per the changed text, this directly contradicts the responsibility outlined in Section 10.1 to provide customer support, particularly if such support extends to end-users under the original, more inclusive definition, thus creating a significant in-text contradiction, following the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1.15 "Hosting Service(s)" shall mean the network hosting (and related) services - as listed in Appendix 1 - provided by T-Mobile to ELEPHANT TALK under the Agreement, enabling ELEPHANT TALK to provide ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services to ELEPHANT TALK Customers in accordance with its terms and conditions of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.15 "Hosting Service(s)" shall mean the network hosting (and related) services - as listed in Appendix 1 - provided by T-Mobile to ELEPHANT TALK under the Agreement, enabling ELEPHANT TALK to provide ELEPHANT TALK Wholesale Services to ELEPHANT TALK Customers, but not necessarily in accordance with all the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition stipulates that T-Mobile's Hosting Services enable ELEPHANT TALK to provide Wholesale Services *in accordance* with the Agreement's terms. The changed definition introduces ambiguity by stating this enablement doesn't necessarily adhere to *all* terms, creating a conflict. This particularly affects section 2.10, creating uncertainty as to whether Hosting Services need to be aligned to the GSM Specifications and the GSM Association Permanent Reference Documents, as defined in the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.10 The Hosting Services shall be provided in accordance with relevant GSM Specifications and the GSM Association Permanent Reference Documents, including all the commercial aspects, as defined in the Agreement. This foregoing is however subject to the additional requirements and exceptions to the GSM specifications and GSM Association Permanent Reference Documents as may be agreed in writing between Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Hosting Services enable ELEPHANT TALK to provide Wholesale Services 'in accordance' with the Agreement's terms. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating that this enablement doesn't necessarily adhere to *all* terms. This directly conflicts with section 2.10, which mandates that 'The Hosting Services shall be provided in accordance with relevant GSM Specifications and the GSM Association Permanent Reference Documents, including all the commercial aspects, as defined in the Agreement'. The modified definition undermines the certainty required by section 2.10, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This is a clear case of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 17.1 This Agreement shall come into force on the Date of the Agreement, and shall, subject to article 18, remain in full force and effect for an initial period of *** from the Commercial Launch Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 17.1 This Agreement shall come into force on the Date of the Agreement, and shall, regardless of article 18, remain in full force and effect for an initial period of *** from the Commercial Launch Date.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause 17.1 states the agreement remains in effect for the initial period, 'subject to article 18' (termination conditions). The change to 'regardless of article 18' creates an ambiguity: does article 18 now have no effect during the initial period? This contradicts article 18.1, where, for example, material breach can cause the agreement to terminate early.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18.1 In addition to the conditions of Article 17, this Agreement may be terminated as follows: a. by mutual agreement of the Parties; or b. by either Party, with immediate effect, if the other Party is in material breach of any provision of this Agreement and does not or is not capable of remedying such breach within a reasonable time and in any event within sixty (60) Business Days of receipt of a written notice to such effect. A material breach will, for the purpose of this Agreement, not include the event that ELEPHANT TALK fails to comply with its payments obligations under article 9.2.; or c. by T-Mobile, if ELEPHANT TALK fails to comply with its obligations under article 16.5 upon written notification thereof by T-Mobile, within a reasonable period of time following the respective notification date (but in no event later than forty-five (45) Business Days of receipt of a written notice to such effect); or, d. by T-Mobile, subject to *** . In the present situation ***; or e. by T-Mobile or its successors, ***.; or, f. by ELEPHANT TALK, subject to *** and this entity is at the time of signing of ***; or g.. by either Party, with immediate effect, if the other Party becomes bankrupt or insolvent (or bankruptcy or insolvency is appropriately requested for) or is liquidated or dissolved other than for the purpose of a bona fide reconstruction or amalgamation, or if that other Party enters into any composition or arrangements with its creditors; or,
perturbation.justification: The original clause 17.1 makes the agreement's initial term subject to the termination conditions in article 18. The changed text explicitly states that article 18 is disregarded during the initial period, which is in direct conflict with article 18.1, which outlines conditions under which the agreement can be terminated, such as a material breach. This change creates an in-text contradiction because it removes the allowance for early termination under specific conditions during the initial period, directly conflicting with the rights and obligations defined in article 18.1, thus fulfilling the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.
